old_claim.note: Department Stores & Mass Retail provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Mechanized production of consumer durables created the product volumes and price structure through which installment selling became available to a broad market.

source_shape.source_locator: The Harvard exhibit says mechanization and mass-produced consumer durables made installment plans broadly available in the nineteenth century and quotes the Singer company plan in 1856.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Harvard exhibit says mechanization and mass production of durable goods made installment plans available to average consumers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The production system explains the volume and economics behind installment contracts, while the cited Singer milestone was not a department-store practice.

ontology_before: consumer_installment_credit inherited from department_stores_mass_retail, which the cited evidence did not establish as its direct enabling method.
ontology_after: consumer_installment_credit inherits from mass_production, the source-documented capability used by the scoped milestone.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mass_production replaces department_stores_mass_retail as a direct prerequisite for consumer_installment_credit.
would_reject_if: Direct evidence showed that department_stores_mass_retail, rather than mass_production, supplied the operative method used in the scoped consumer_installment_credit milestone.
short_reason: Harvard ties broad installment selling to mass-produced durable goods, not department-store origin.
